test_avg__mae_surf_p: NaN (test_geom_camber_cruise sample 20 has Inf in y[p] volume nodes; surface p finite per-split: see comment)

note: 23% worse than huber-loss baseline; close as standalone. Direction (per-channel surf weighting) still has merit; revisit on top of huber baseline if other levers stall.
depth-8 only completes 9/50 epochs at 30-min budget (epoch ~206s). Hypothesis not falsified, but compute-infeasible at this depth; cosine LR schedule barely engaged. Close.

+22% vs huber baseline 105.999. Standalone dropout-on-MSE didn't beat huber-no-dropout. Direction not falsified relative to merged huber baseline; revisit MLP-dropout or dropout+huber stacking if needed.
+46% vs huber baseline; slice-128 also reduced budget (170 s/epoch -> 11 epochs vs ~14 at slice-64). Closing standalone; revisit if Huber+slice-128 ever explored.

+16.2% vs huber baseline 105.999. 100% of batches were clipped at max_norm=1.0 (pre-clip mean 30-200) — effective LR was much smaller than configured 1e-3 peak. Recipe was masked by the clip; round-2 retry should drop or loosen the clip.

Metric-pipeline fix; val drift of +1.99% vs huber baseline (105.999) is RNG noise (workaround did NOT trigger on any val sample, only on test_geom_camber_cruise sample 20). Test_avg recovered from NaN to 97.957 — paper-facing metric is now finite for the first time on this branch.

test_avg__mae_surf_p: NaN (PR #361 not merged before this run; cruise sample 20 Inf)
note: -4.39% vs huber baseline (105.999 -> 101.350). EMA completely damped the round-1 transient spike; val curve was monotonically decreasing every epoch. Biggest gains: cruise (-6.93%), single_in_dist (-5.76%), re_rand (-5.19%). camber_rc flat (-0.07%). New baseline.

+2.10% vs huber baseline 105.999. Helped only single_in_dist (-7.45%); hurt all 3 OOD splits (+5-7%). Volume MAE regressed +10-17% across splits — sw=25 starves the volume term too much. Direction not falsified at lower bumps; revisit sw in [12, 15] later if needed.

+9.8% vs huber baseline (105.999), +14.8% vs new EMA baseline (101.350). Validated hypothesis 1 (clip was redundant under huber - grad norms decayed smoothly 56->9 mean over 14 epochs, no instability). But hypothesis 2 (recipe gives -3 to -8%) didn't pan out: T_max=47 sized for 50 epochs but only 14 ran, so the model trained almost entirely at near-peak lr (~0.9 of 1e-3) without ever decaying. Test_avg=105.715 is finite (surface MAE didn't see the cruise volume Inf).

test_avg__mae_surf_p: NaN (#361 not yet landed when run started)
note: +1.81% vs huber baseline 105.999, +6.5% vs EMA baseline 101.350. Mechanism worked (Ux/Uy degraded more than p in relative terms), but absolute mae_surf_p got worse not better. Combined with round-1 PR #295 ([1,1,2.5]) also regressing, channel weighting on huber direction is now thoroughly falsified. Drop direction.

+2.96% vs huber 105.999, +7.7% vs EMA 101.350. Direction has structured signal: val_single_in_dist (highest target dynamic range, matches hypothesis) -3.86% val / -8.06% test — large hypothesis-aligned win. But cruise +11.81% and re_rand +6.88% (which the hypothesis predicted would IMPROVE) regressed. Student's diagnosis: 8 geometric bands up to 2^7=128 rad is too aggressive for a 4-decade log(Re) signal — highest frequencies produce per-Re fingerprints rather than smooth Re-functions, hurting cross-Re generalization. Aliasing failure mode. Salvageable with narrower band.

-12.95% vs EMA baseline (101.350 -> 88.227). Massive win on val_avg, -20.03% on test_avg (97.957 -> 78.338). Param-matched (+1.3% n_params). All 4 val splits and all 4 test splits improved. The previously-flat val_geom_camber_rc finally moved (-8.23% vs EMA's -0.07%). Loss curve still descending at the cap - undertrained, more epochs would likely improve further. New baseline.

+7.1% vs EMA baseline (101.350 -> 108.558). Equal-epoch (ep13 vs ep13): +2.6% (105.832 vs 108.558) - 50% more params yielded NEGATIVE marginal returns. Per-split signature CONTRADICTS capacity-bottleneck hypothesis: highest-MAE splits (single_in_dist, camber_rc) regressed MOST (+8.83%, +8.18%). Diagnosis: bottleneck on those splits is generalization, not raw FFN capacity. Single-axis FFN scaling is dead.

Branched off pre-EMA but rebased onto post-EMA advisor. -3.43% vs original huber baseline (105.999 -> 102.359, matches predicted -3 to -8% range), but +1.00% vs new EMA baseline (101.350). Test_avg=93.052 (-5.0% vs PR #361 finite test 97.957). Student diagnosis: cosine T_max=14 + EMA(0.999) interact non-additively. EMA half-life ~1.85 epochs at our batch count; with T_max=14 the last 5-7 epochs run at lr<28% of peak so EMA averages near-stillness. Cruise (+6.20%) and re_rand (+2.21%) regressed, dominating val_avg loss. Both levers individually good; not orthogonal. Now further behind the SwiGLU baseline (88.227).

+4.18% vs EMA baseline (101.350); +19.7% vs SwiGLU baseline (88.227). All 4 val splits regressed (+2.5 to +5.9%); val_geom_camber_rc - the canary split for the capacity hypothesis - regressed MOST (+5.86%). Cleanly falsifies the shared-head-as-capacity-bottleneck hypothesis. Removing the shared projection's implicit cross-channel coupling appears to hurt more than per-channel specialization helps.

+6.18% vs EMA baseline; +21.97% vs SwiGLU baseline. All 4 val splits regressed (+2.5 to +8.5%); OOD splits regressed worst. Curve was monotonically decreasing (smoothness prediction confirmed) but absolute level worse. Student's analysis: at the high-error regime we're stuck in (val going 328->108 in 14 epochs), delta=2's quadratic region for |err| in [1,2] underweights moderate errors compared to delta=1's bounded gradient. delta=1 sits at a sweet spot.

Branched at start of round 1 (pre-huber). +61.5% vs SwiGLU baseline 88.227. Compute-infeasible: only 8/50 epochs at 240s/epoch. Val curve was still in steep descent at the cap (epoch 7->8: 166->142). Hypothesis (capacity-medium) NOT testable in 8 epochs - same compute-infeasibility issue as thorfinn's closed depth-8 PR. Student independently rediscovered the NaN propagation bug and implemented a byte-identical fix to PR #361 (already on advisor branch). Diligent work despite the budget constraint.

-5.67% vs SwiGLU baseline (88.227 -> 83.223). Materially larger than predicted (-0.5 to -2%). All 4 val splits and all 4 test splits improved uniformly (3.78-7.13% on val, 4.16-6.85% on test). Mechanism (per student): the dominant effect is EMA bias correction during the under-trained cold start, NOT cosine-tail interaction as originally predicted. EMA(0.999) is heavily anchored to random init for the first ~2 epochs (half-life ~1.85ep); EMA(0.99) tracks the live model immediately (half-life ~0.18ep). New baseline.

+2.35% vs SwiGLU baseline. val_single_in_dist improved (-2.05%) but OOD splits regressed (+5-7%). Pattern: head SwiGLU is more expressive on familiar samples but generalizes worse to OOD. Student diagnosis: head has no residual connection (unlike per-block FFN which sits inside +fx residual), so SwiGLU's gating non-linearity acts unbuffered. 3x param count without enough data within 13 epochs. Closing.

Branched on EMA pre-SwiGLU. +1.54% vs EMA baseline (101.350); +16.6% vs current SwiGLU baseline (88.227). Aliasing diagnosis partially validated: cruise +6.19% (recovered ~70% from bands=8's +20.1%); re_rand +2.67% (recovered ~80% from +12.7%). val_single_in_dist retained -1.07% (attenuated from -3.86% at bands=8) — low-frequency content carries the bulk but not all of the win. test_avg=93.217 (4-split finite) beats prior huber 4-split test (97.957) by -4.84% — but doesn't beat SwiGLU baseline 78.338. Closing standalone; Fourier direction has signal but doesn't compound to beat the SwiGLU lever.

Null result vs SwiGLU baseline (-0.11% on val, +1.06% on test - both within run-to-run noise). Vs current EMA(0.99) baseline 83.223: +5.9%. Trajectory statistically indistinguishable from SwiGLU baseline at every epoch. SiLU vs GELU at typical post-LayerNorm magnitudes are too close to separate at this scale. Activation choice is below the noise floor for 0.67M params and 1499 train samples.

Falsified hypothesis. +1.76% vs SwiGLU (88.227); +8.1% vs EMA(0.99) (83.223). The two splits flagged as generalization-bottlenecked (camber_rc, re_rand) BOTH got worse, not better - exact opposite of prediction. Excellent student diagnosis: half the fun-feature channels (Re, AoA, NACA, gap, stagger) are CONSTANT per sample, so randn_like noise gives different 'Reynolds number' to each node within the same forward pass - destroys the (geometry, flow conditions) -> field map. Per-node noise scheme is structurally wrong for this data; per-sample noise on constant dims would be the right test (queued as PR #460).

Branched on SwiGLU pre-EMA(0.99). val_avg=87.265: -1.1% vs SwiGLU baseline (88.227); +4.9% vs current EMA(0.99) baseline (83.223). 3/4 val splits improved; cruise regressed slightly (+1.22). delta profile is monotonic toward L1: delta=2 gave 107.609, delta=1 gave 88.227, delta=0.5 gives 87.265 - diminishing returns (~19 pts gained from delta=2->1, ~1 pt from delta=1->0.5). Curve very smooth, strictly monotonic - no late-training instability seen at delta=0.5. Doesn't beat current baseline standalone, but useful information; queued follow-up to delta=0.25 stacked with EMA(0.99).

+1.71% vs EMA(0.99) baseline (83.223). Cold start improved (ep1: 183 vs EMA(0.99)'s 193) but rest regressed. Single & camber_rc improved slightly; cruise +7.04% and re_rand +4.19% regressed. Diagnosis: bias correction reaches effective decay 0.977 by step 375, 0.995 by 1875 — outside first ~50 steps, scheme is essentially EMA(0.999) which we know is worse here. Dominant effect of EMA(0.99) was CONTINUOUS fast tracking through the under-trained 13-epoch budget, not just cold-start bias. The two effects don't separate cleanly in this regime.

Rebased onto SwiGLU baseline (88.227); +40.5% vs SwiGLU; +49% vs current EMA(0.99) baseline (83.223). Diagnosis: grad-accum-2 halves optimizer step count while keeping wall-clock fixed — under 13-epoch timeout-bound regime where val is still descending fast (~5 pts/epoch), halving updates is catastrophic. EMA also accumulates half as many ticks. Per-step efficiency only matters at converged training; we never reach that here. Curve was monotone (smoothness held), but absolute level ~ epoch 6.5 of baseline trajectory. Hypothesis would only land at longer wall-clock budget where step-count parity is achievable.

-3.30% vs EMA(0.99) baseline 83.223 -> 80.480; test_avg -2.13% (73.904 -> 72.328). Param-identical (no new learnable params). Effective drop probs [0, 0.025, 0.05, 0.075, 0.0] - small but real. Curve was parallel to baseline with a uniform offset (not a late-training kick). Did NOT preferentially help OOD-camber splits as predicted (camber_rc -1.12% near noise; re_rand flat -0.08%); biggest wins on val_single_in_dist (-5.98%) and val_geom_camber_cruise (-6.41%). NEW BASELINE.

+0.39% vs EMA(0.99) baseline (83.223). Per-split asymmetric: helps harder splits (single_in_dist -2.25%, camber_rc -0.76%); hurts easier (cruise +5.09%, re_rand +1.48%). Net near-tie. Excellent diagnosis: gammas DID grow 50-400x from 1e-4 init (gamma_attn 5e-3 to 1.3e-2; gamma_mlp 2.5e-2 to 3.8e-2 by ep13) but still 25-200x smaller than 1.0 - never entered the 'specialization regime' where LayerScale's value lies. Under our 13-epoch budget, near-identity-at-init silences early-epoch signal without enough time to mature. Direction not dead at larger init or longer budget; queued: layerscale_init=1e-2 as direct follow-up.

Branched on SwiGLU pre-EMA(0.99). +3.53% vs SwiGLU baseline (epoch 13 vs ep 12); +9.8% vs current EMA(0.99) baseline. **Per-step quality won at every checked epoch** (RMSNorm vs LayerNorm: ep1 320 vs 333, ep5 169 vs 182, ep12 91.34 vs 92.27 - RMSNorm always equal-or-better). BUT torch.nn.RMSNorm in PyTorch 2.10 is 16.9% SLOWER per epoch than nn.LayerNorm (162.4s vs 138.9s) - kernel-dispatch issue with the ATen op. Cost ~1 epoch under cap. Hypothesis qualitatively validated; implementation choice killed wall-clock. Strong follow-up: re-run with manual nn.Module RMSNorm.

MAJOR WIN: -13.0% vs EMA(0.99)+SwiGLU baseline (83.223 -> 72.414); test_avg -14.6% (73.904 -> 63.082). All 4 val splits improved 10-18%. Cruise canary (regressed at delta=0.5) gained MOST at delta=0.25 (-17.88%). Profile DID NOT saturate at delta=0.5 - profile is still descending steeply. Run was branched pre-DropPath; merging onto post-DropPath advisor stack.

Diagnosis from PR #425 confirmed. -2.15% vs EMA(0.99)+SwiGLU baseline (83.223), but +1.19% vs current DropPath baseline (80.480). Splits broken by per-node noise (PR #425) recovered: camber_rc -3.27% (was +6.57%), re_rand flat (was +9.18%). Direction validated but doesn't beat current standalone. Could compound on huber(0.25)+DropPath baseline; queued for resweep.

+6.1% vs EMA(0.99)+SwiGLU baseline; +9.7% vs current DropPath baseline (80.480). 3/4 splits regressed 8-10%. Trajectory matched baseline through epoch 5 then diverged as fine-detail learning kicks in. Diagnosis: SwiGLU gating at input projection prunes raw physical input channels (Re, AoA, NACA, etc.) BEFORE mixing - destroys irreducible physical signal. Per-block SwiGLU gates AFTER mixing where some channels really are redundant. Direction is dead - 'LLaMA-everywhere' breaks at the input layer.

Wall-clock recovered partially (157.89s vs nn.RMSNorm 162.4s, -2.8%) but did NOT reach LayerNorm parity (138.9s, +13.7% off). Got 12 epochs vs baseline's 13. val_avg=84.149: +4.6% vs DropPath baseline (80.480), +16.2% vs current huber-d=0.25 (72.414). Excellent diagnosis: train.py runs eager mode (no torch.compile), so TorchInductor was never invoked. RMSNorm dispatches into 4-5 kernels per call; LayerNorm has a fused CUDA kernel. Manual nn.Module won't reach parity in eager mode regardless of implementation. Direction needs torch.compile or a custom Triton kernel.

MAJOR LEVER: -6.34% vs the EMA(0.99)+SwiGLU baseline she branched on (83.223 -> 77.951); test_avg -6.97% (73.904 -> 68.753). Predicted -0.5% to -1.5%; actual -6.34% (4-13x predicted). Mechanism: beta_2=0.95 (half-life ~14 steps) tracks the cosine-decay-shaped gradient surface much better than beta_2=0.999 (half-life ~700 steps) inside the 13-epoch under-trained regime where beta_2=0.999 is anchored to random gradients for the first 2 epochs. Trajectory: matched baseline ep1-3, opened ~3-6% lead from ep5 onward. ORTHOGONAL to fern's huber-delta=0.25 (different mechanism: optimizer 2nd-moment vs loss curvature). Doesn't beat current standalone (77.951 > 72.414) but mergeable as orthogonal compound.

Branched on EMA(0.99)+SwiGLU pre-DropPath. -2.37% vs the EMA(0.99)+SwiGLU baseline (83.223 -> 81.251); test_avg -1.82% (73.904 -> 72.560). Both diagnostics passed: cold-start gain transferred (epoch-1 ~186 vs EMA(0.99)'s ~193), AND fast-tracking preserved (cruise +0.47%, re_rand +0.52% - flat as predicted, NOT regressed like PR #454's bias-corrected EMA(0.999) was). Mechanism: warmup ramp (1+t)/(10+t) keeps decay <0.99 for first ~50 steps then asymptotes. Strict superset of current EMA(0.99). MERGE as orthogonal compound.

+1.04% vs DropPath baseline (80.480 -> 81.314); +12.3% vs current 72.414. Two LayerScale init values (1e-4 in PR #456 and 1e-2 here) both regressed - LayerScale gating direction now firmly disconfirmed. Critical falsification: gammas equilibrate at ~0.01-0.05 regardless of init (1e-4 grew 250x to ~0.025; 1e-2 grew only 0.65-4.8x to similar absolute values). Trip changes but DESTINATION doesn't. Per-split asymmetry preserved (helps single/camber_rc, hurts cruise/re_rand) - structural property of LayerScale on this dataset, not init-dependent transient. Drop direction.

+0.47% vs DropPath baseline (80.480 -> 80.858); test essentially at parity (+0.10%). Curve faster start (epochs 1-3) but lags mid (epochs 6-12); converges at epoch 14. DropPath 0.1 -> 0.2 SATURATED at the 14-epoch budget. val_re_rand bright spot (-4.55%) but other splits regressed enough to net out. Could potentially win at longer epoch budget (curves still converging at the cap).

delta=0.1 ties delta=0.25 within noise: -0.06% val, -0.30% test. Profile has flattened. delta=1->0.25 gave -10.8 absolute; delta=0.25->0.1 gives -0.045. Per-split mixed: 3/4 splits modestly improve, val_geom_camber_rc regresses +3.5% (only split that does). No L1-style instability. delta profile is now EXHAUSTED. Don't merge - within run-to-run noise. Move to architecture-side levers.

+3.36% vs reference 72.414 (74.8448); +5.10% test. Asymmetry signal: damage concentrated on in-dist splits (single +5.48%, camber_rc +5.60%) while harder OOD splits stayed flat. 0.02 noise over-regularizes on top of DropPath + delta=0.25. Three-regularizer stack past optimum. Don't sweep up; sweep DOWN (0.005-0.015) is the natural follow-up.

+1.87% vs reference 72.414 (73.771); +1.35% test. 7/8 metrics regressed; only val_re_rand improved (-1.0%). Curve more conservative descent. Diagnosis: extra L2 shrinkage hurts deeper/wider weights (FFN intermediate=176, attn proj at 128) which are already under-trained at 14 epochs. wd=3e-4 over-regularizes on top of DropPath + delta=0.25. The under-regularized direction (wd=3e-5 or 1e-5) is the orthogonal lever still untested.

+0.74% vs 72.414 reference; +1.08% test. 6/8 splits slightly worse; only re_rand modestly improves. Predicted noise/instability didn't materialize - curve fully monotone, 0.90's ~7-step half-life is well-behaved. Mechanism: beta_2=0.95 was already in the regime tracking cosine-shaped gradient well in 14 epochs; pushing to 0.90 just gives up tiny variance reduction without a tracking benefit. beta_2 profile saturated at 0.95.

WIN: -0.99% val (72.414 -> 71.6985), -0.79% test (63.082 -> 62.5824). First PR to MEASURABLY beat the merged baseline. Final per-block temperatures all converged to [0.95, 0.99] - confirms 0.5 init was 2x below optimum. Mechanism: optimization-warmup phenomenon, not capacity. Init=1.0 starts at optimum so gradients fit data instead of un-doing init. Per-split: single_in_dist gains most (-2.77%/-4.63%); test_geom_camber_rc regressed +3.84% (only split going backward). Param-identical, zero compute cost.

WIN: -1.23% vs current 71.6985 baseline (val_avg=70.814); -2.21% vs 72.414 reference. wd profile: 3e-5 (70.814) < 1e-4 (72.414) < 3e-4 (73.771) — monotonically decreasing. The merged stack is OVER-REGULARIZED at wd=1e-4. 3/4 val splits improve; val_geom_camber_cruise +1.86% (only regression). Test_avg essentially flat (+0.71% vs current). MERGE as orthogonal compound to slice-temp.

+12.3% vs current 71.6985 baseline. Critical infra finding: param count was NOT identical to baseline — actually -16,620 params (-2.48%) because PhysicsAttention's to_q/to_k/to_v are head-SHARED Linear(dim_head, dim_head) projections, not per-head. So shrinking dim_head 32->16 cut 4x per layer. Wall-clock +25% (173s vs 138s) — got 11 epochs vs 14. Per-epoch convergence FASTER (val=80.5 at ep10 vs 81.3 at ep13 baseline) but timeout cuts it short. Per-split signature: helps cruise/re_rand, hurts single_in_dist. Architectural signal real but masked by wall-clock penalty.

WIN: -0.38% vs current 71.6985 baseline (val_avg=71.4284); -1.36% vs 72.414 reference. ALL 4 val splits improved. 0.99 asymptote NOT load-bearing: cruise -2.06% and re_rand -3.14% (the splits flat under warmup=10 became the biggest improvers under warmup=50). Effective decay never reaches 0.99 in budget (max 0.988 at step 4000). Test_avg slight regression (+1.37% vs current). MERGE as orthogonal compound.

MAJOR WIN: -7.05% val (72.414 -> 67.306) and -6.00% test (63.082 -> 59.296). 1-ep linear warmup + cosine T_max=13 = total 14 epochs, schedule lands exactly at the timeout. ALL 4 val splits improved 3-12%; single_in_dist biggest win (-11.66%). LR trajectory confirmed: ep1 ramps to 2.5e-4, ep2 hits peak 5e-4, ep14 lands at ~7e-6 (close to zero). Late-epoch slope shallowed from 4.3 -> 0.7 pts/epoch - clear 'fine-tuning regime' signature. The merged stack provides enough basin geometry that proper cosine decay actually bites (askeladd's PR #370 T_max=14 regressed +1% on EMA(0.99)+SwiGLU only because that stack already saturated the fast-tracking benefit).

WIN on val: -1.46% vs 72.414 reference; -0.47% vs current 71.6985. test_avg +0.65% (slight regression). Sweep DOWN profile working: noise std=0.02 saturated (PR #495 +3.36%), std=0.005 wins. Biggest wins on val_re_rand (-3.45%) and val_geom_camber_rc (-1.83%) - splits that 0.02 hurt most. Confirms saturation at 0.02; merged-stack optimum is in (0, 0.01]. MERGE as orthogonal compound.
